title: Problem 1: Equivalence of $\Phi^4_3$ Measure under Shift
author: Martin Hairer (EPFL and Imperial)
statement_excerpt: Let $\mathbb{T}^3$ be the three dimensional unit size torus and let $\mu$ be the $\Phi^4_3$ measure on the space of distributions $\mathcal{D}'(\mathbb{T}^3)$. Let $\psi : \mathbb{T}^3 \to \mathbb{R}$ be a smooth function that is not identically zero and let $T_\psi : \mathcal{D}'(\mathbb{T}^3) \to \mathcal{D}'(\mathbb{T}^3)$ be the shift map given by $T_\psi(u) = u + \psi$ (with the usual identification of smooth functions as distributions). Are the measures $\mu$ and $T_\psi^* \mu$ equivalent? Here, equivalence of measures is in the sense of having the same null sets and $T_\psi^*$ denotes the pushforward under $T_\psi$.
normalized_statement: Let $\mathbb{T}^3$ be the three dimensional unit size torus and let $\mu$ be the $\Phi^4_3$ measure on the space of distributions $\mathcal{D}'(\mathbb{T}^3)$. Are the measures $\mu$ and $T_\psi^* \mu$ equivalent? Here, equivalence of measures is in the sense of having the same null sets and $T_\psi^*$ denotes the pushforward under $T_\psi$.
